title: Alatiglossum ciliatum
taxonomy: Alatiglossum ciliatum belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or Indian paintbrushes, which are hemiparasitic plants. These plants are often parasitic on other plants but can also photosynthesize like normal plants. Alatiglossum ciliatum is native to South America, specifically in the Andean region, where it grows in rocky and grassy areas. As a member of the Orobanchaceae family, Alatiglossum ciliatum is closely related to other similar plants like Castilleja and Pedicularis. Further classification of Alatiglossum ciliatum can be found within the subfamily Orobanchoideae.
